verses.1: Why do You stand far away, LORD? Why do You hide Yourself in times of trouble?
verses.2: In arrogance the wicked hotly pursue the needy; Let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
verses.3: For the wicked boasts of his soul’s desire, And the greedy person curses and shows disrespect to the LORD.
verses.4: The wicked, in his haughtiness, does not seek Him. There is no God in all his schemes.
verses.5: His ways succeed at all times; Yet Your judgments are on high, out of his sight; As for all his enemies, he snorts at them.
verses.6: He says to himself, “I will not be moved; Throughout the generations I will not be in adversity.”
verses.7: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression; Under his tongue is harm and injustice.
verses.8: He sits in the lurking places of the villages; He kills the innocent in the secret places; His eyes surreptitiously watch for the unfortunate.
verses.9: He lurks in secret like a lion in his lair; He lurks to catch the needy; He catches the needy when he pulls him into his net.
verses.10: Then he crushes the needy one, who cowers; And unfortunate people fall by his mighty power.
verses.11: He says to himself, “God has forgotten; He has hidden His face; He will never see it.”
verses.12: Arise, LORD; God, lift up Your hand. Do not forget the humble.
verses.13: Why has the wicked treated God disrespectfully? He has said to himself, “You will not require an account. ”
verses.14: You have seen it, for You have looked at harm and provocation to take it into Your hand. The unfortunate commits himself to You; You have been the helper of the orphan.
verses.15: Break the arm of the wicked and the evildoer, Seek out his wickedness until You find none.
verses.16: The LORD is King forever and ever; Nations have perished from His land.
verses.17: LORD, You have heard the desire of the humble; You will strengthen their heart, You will make Your ear attentive
verses.18: To vindicate the orphan and the oppressed, So that mankind, which is of the earth, will no longer cause terror.
